header_summary:
The proposed modification to BIP360 aims to enhance the privacy and security of Bitcoin scripting by mandating a minimum of one merkle authentication path in P2MR transactions and prohibiting depth-zero script trees. This change, which prevents the use of less secure script methods, is expected to align P2MR's functionality with P2TR, promoting the use of more robust scripting practices such as adding a post-quantum cryptography leaf to standard EC Schnorr leaves. This initiative has been positively received by several protocol proponents, though further consultations are sought, particularly with those focusing on P2TRv2, to finalize the impact on privacy and UTXO set fungibility [here](https://gnusha.org/pi/bitcoindev/Q8YTY1ArMzia7tRvcZ5v769WPxCMxAl_0rUy-byOBjBcTd4HXj7IB7Yt4gxLbKqk7EXFWy-PuGB6QtI28qRjE5Vob-l44UmBH6L8aXInoSk=@proton.me/T/#u#m48f1bda89d1f1eeb5234f1524176ec48f8d5d7e3).

In another development, the P2WOTS initiative marks a significant advancement in the post-quantum security of Bitcoin, demonstrated through extensive testing and community involvement. The proposal is accessible for public testing via a custom signet and wallet interface, with further details and ongoing updates available on the project’s GitHub repository. This initiative highlights the community-driven approach to enhancing Bitcoin's security against quantum threats and encourages broad participation to ensure its viability and integration into the Bitcoin protocol [here](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/p2wots-live-signet-interactive-wallet-demo/2557).

Additionally, there's a discussion about the introduction of a new output type focused on ML-DSA key commitment, raising concerns about its cryptographic flexibility and potential security vulnerabilities. Critics argue that the choice of ML-DSA-65 over ML-DSA-44, despite its higher resource consumption, lacks sufficient justification and that alternatives like SLH-DSA might offer more efficient and secure solutions. The proposal also faces scalability issues due to the absence of witness discounts, which could significantly lower transaction throughput, highlighting the need for a more balanced approach to implementing post-quantum cryptographic methods in Bitcoin [here](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/bip-draft-witness-version-3-ml-dsa-65-post-quantum-key-path-spending/2422/3).

recent_posts.title: Aligning privacy incentives in P2MR
recent_posts.summary: - The proposed BIP360 aims to mandate a merkle authentication path in P2MR control blocks.  - This change seeks to eliminate depth-zero script trees, thus enhancing script privacy and efficiency.  - The proposal received initial support, with further input sought from P2TRv2 proponents.

recent_posts.title: Prohibit Merkle Internal Node Preimages That Encode Minimal 64-Byte Transactions
recent_posts.summary:
- The rule targets merkle root validity to prevent prefix attacks in second-layer clients.
- AI aided in drafting a Bitcoin Improvement Proposal, focusing on refining accuracy.
- This approach upholds transparency and critically reviews the AI's contributions.

recent_posts.title: BIP 3 and issues on BIPs
recent_posts.summary:
- The recent review clarifies that "open issues" in Bitcoin Proposals are minor.
- Tags like "todo" in BIPs indicate placeholders, not significant problems.
- Misunderstandings may result from analyses of unpublished, not formal, BIP drafts.

recent_posts.title: P2WOTS: Live Signet & Interactive Wallet Demo
recent_posts.summary:
- The P2WOTS initiative enhances Bitcoin security against quantum threats via a lightweight design.
- Comprehensive tests confirm P2WOTS' effectiveness; public can interact via an online demo and signet.
- Community engagement and feedback are vital for promoting P2WOTS' integration into Bitcoin protocol.

recent_posts.title: [BIP Draft] Witness Version 3: ML-DSA-65 Post-Quantum Key-Path Spending
recent_posts.summary: - The proposal introduces ML-DSA key commitment, limiting cryptographic flexibility.  - ML-DSA-65 is chosen over ML-DSA-44 without clear justification for higher resource use.  - Alternatives like SLH-DSA or XMSS may offer better security and efficiency, questioning ML-DSA's preference.

recent_posts.title: Bird of Prey 2: non-malleable Schnorr + PQ signatures
recent_posts.summary:
- The significance of SUF in cryptocurrency has reduced with the implementation of segwit.
- Vulnerabilities in hybrid cryptographic schemes risk system integrity if any component fails.
- Boris Nagaev proposes integrating BIP340 with a hash-based scheme to save space.

active_posts.title: PQC - What is our Goal, Even?
active_posts.summary: - The PQC community experiences disagreements about securing Bitcoin against quantum threats.  - Popular wallets like Bitcoin.com increase risk by not prioritizing advanced security features.  - Focus on immediate security needs over developing future quantum-resistant technologies is advised.

active_posts.title: PQC: Lattice-based signatures
active_posts.summary: - Nikita's discussion on PQC debates the efficacy of lattice-based signatures.  - Concerns are raised about continuing the focus on traditional hash-based cryptography.  - The community is urged to evaluate the completeness of PQC comparison tables.

active_posts.title: [BIP Draft] Testnet 5
active_posts.summary:
- A new Testnet5 draft seeks to improve from Testnet4 challenges.
- It integrates usability fixes and tests BIP54 concurrently.
- Feedback is requested via GitHub or the Bitcoin Development Mailing List.

active_posts.title: BIP-110 update: v0.4.1 release and implementation submitted to Bitcoin Core
active_posts.summary: - Version 0.4.1 of BIP-110 aims to enhance Bitcoin by limiting transaction data sizes.  - Stability and amended consensus rules mark v0.4.1's readiness for potential December 2025 activation.  - Community and platform engagement escalate as BIP-110 integrates into more Bitcoin infrastructure.

active_posts.title: P2WOTS: Post Quantum UTXO Winternitz Signatures
active_posts.summary:
- The proposal introduces lightweight Winternitz signatures for Bitcoin's UTXO model.
- It features a new Bitcoin output type to enhance security and compatibility.
- Post-quantum security is not extended to multi-signature configurations.

active_posts.title: CTV-only Vault Concept v0.1.0 release
active_posts.summary:
- The discussion examines OP_TEMPLATEHASH's effectiveness and compatibility.
- It questions if OP_TEMPLATEHASH can match current methods and tools.
- The inquiry assesses potential issues in prioritizing OP_TEMPLATEHASH over alternatives.

today_in_history_posts.title: UHS: Full-node security without maintaining a full UTXO set
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- The proposal reduces pruned node size by 40% but increases bandwidth by 5%.
- Implementing the change won't permanently alter the blockchain structure.
- Testing is needed to assess RAM efficiency improvements on low-end devices.

today_in_history_posts.title: New idea on decentralized identity and truth (Re: Numerifides)
today_in_history_posts.summary: - The author suggests a new decentralized identity system using Lightning.  - Nodes can query and respond to requests, with costs based on query popularity and length.  - Future enhancements may include commercial queries, funded by companies or enthusiasts.

today_in_history_posts.title: Thoughts on scaling and consensus changes (2023)
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin scaling involves creating 50,000 off-chain "bitcoin banks" for better transaction handling.
- These entities, interconnected for liquidity, balance transaction affordability with security risks.
- Off-chain solutions incorporate tools like CheckTemplateVerify for managing congestion efficiently.
